DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-26-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Elizabeth A. Hernandez, Esq., of BD&J, PC., Tel:(310) 887-1818 for Plaintiff's.
Antigone Montgomery, Esq., of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP for Defendant Securitas.
Court Reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR #13717, Tel: 760-805-3974 email: RyanWheeler91@yahoo.com.

Hearing held: A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff's counsel regarding the demurrer based on failure to state a cause of action is sustained. Plaintiffs fail to allege a private fact or a highly offensive disclosure that lacked a legitimate public concern. (Brown v. Metro by T-Mobile (2024) 768 F.Supp.3d 1068, 1085 [public disclosure of private facts claim failed where passive security-camera footage captured a violent crime on a public street (shooting)].) Plaintiffs' reliance on Catsouras v. Department of California Highway Patrol (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 856 is misplaced. There, the defendants disseminated graphic photographs of a decedent who had been decapitated in an automobile accident. The images were disclosed for "pure morbidity and sensationalism without legitimate public interests or law enforcement purpose." (Id. at 874; see also Gill v. Hearst Pub. Co., 40 Cal.2d at 231 ["Such situation is readily distinguishable from cases where the right of privacy has been enforced with regard to the publication of a picture which was shocking, revolting or indecent in its portrayal of the human body."].) In this case, while the stabbing was certainly tragic, the complaint does not describe similarly graphic images lacking a public purpose.

The matter argue, and upon conclusion of the hearing, the Court adopted the tentative.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Defendant's (Securitas Security Services USA, Inc.) demurrer to the sixth cause of action for invasion of privacy (public disclosure of private facts) is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).)

Order Signed.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield, Clerk: M. Macadangdang,Court Reporter: Ryan Wheeler, CSR #13717, Tel: 760-805-3974 email: RyanWheeler91@yahoo.com.Reported. (302/CM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Criminal Case
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to Open court, the clerk informs the court there are parties appearing remotely in an abundance of caution since Mr. Momeni is incarcerated. The court did not receive any notice to contest the TR.

Appearances (by Zoom):
Ashley Akhavan, Esq. 310-424-5349 BD & J for Plaintiff.
Alexander Farkas, Esq. 949-336-1591 Schafer mcMahon, LLP for Defendant Elyassnia M.D.
No Appearance by Defendant Nima Momeni (SF #665903) SF Jail #2 415-553-1430.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. Counsel appear in an abundance of caution since Mr. Momeni is incarcerated and may not have any access to the hearing. The Court does not call the matter and informs parties the Court is adopting the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NIMA MOMENI AN INDIVIDUAL (SF #665903) (PROVIDE ACCESS) Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Criminal Case.

Defendant's (NIMA MOMENI) motion to stay this action is denied.

In Avant! Corp v. Sup. Crt, (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 876, 885, the court listed the following factors when ruling on the instant motion: (1) defendant's Fifth Amendment interest; (2) the interest of plaintiff in proceeding expeditiously and conducting discovery and the potential prejudice to plaintiffs of a delay; (3) the convenience of the court in the management of its cases, and the efficient use of judicial resources; (4) the interests of persons not parties to the civil litigation; and (5) the interest of the public in the pending civil and criminal litigation.

Here, these factors weigh in favor of denying this motion. A blanket stay of the entire action as to all defendants would unduly prejudice plaintiffs. It is unclear whether defendant is requesting a stay through potential habeas relief and prosecution of this action against numerous of the co-defendants raises no Fifth Amendment issue. The court notes that defendant has already admitted to stabbing decedent and was convicted of murder. (Hernandez Decl., par. 4.) This case is in its early stages of discovery. (Id. at 9.) The court concludes that defendant can invoke his Fifth Amendment privilege as to particular discovery questions and the broad open-ended stay request goes too far. (Fuller v. Superior Court (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 299, 307-308.) "The trial court must be given the opportunity to determine whether particular questions posed in the depositions would elicit answers that support a conviction or that furnish a link in the chain of evidence needed to prosecute the witness, and which may thus be subject to constitutional protection." (Id. at 308.)

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Elizabeth A. Hernandez, Esq., of BD&J, P.C., Tel:(310) 887-1818 , for plaintiffs THE ESTATE OF ROBERT HAROLD LEE, by and through its personal representative, TIMOTHY OLIVER LEE; DAGNY LEE, an individual; and SCOUT LEE, a minor, by and through her Guardian Ad Litem, KRISTA LEE
Alexander M. Farkas, Esq., of SCHAFER McMAHON, LLP., Tel:(949) 727-7077 for defendants Dino Elyassnia, M.D., Khazar Momeni, and Mahnaz Tayarani
Court Reporter: Nancy Presant CSR #: 9906 Email: npresantcsr@gmail.com, Tel:510-757-8550

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff's counsel pertaining to the court not granting leave to amend the Fifth Cause of Action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress (IIED).The matter was argued on the record. Having reviewed the pleadings, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer as to Causes of Action 3 (Aiding and Abetting), 4 (Conspiracy), and 5 (Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress) is sustained. Leave to amend is granted as to COA 3 and 4 and denied as to COA 5. The issue under section CCP 430.10(e) is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a claim for relief. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court liberally construes the complaint pursuant to CCP 452.

(See word minutes for coplete detail)

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang;Court Reporter: Nancy Presant CSR #: 9906 Email: npresantcsr@gmail.com, Tel:510-757-8550. Reported. (302/SKF)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Elizabeth A. Hernandez, Esq., of BD&J, P.C., Tel:(310) 887-1818 , for plaintiffs THE ESTATE OF ROBERT HAROLD LEE, by and through its personal representative, TIMOTHY OLIVER LEE; DAGNY LEE, an individual; and SCOUT LEE, a minor, by and through her Guardian Ad Litem, KRISTA LEE
Alexander M. Farkas, Esq., of SCHAFER McMAHON, LLP., Tel:(949) 727-7077 for defendants Dino Elyassnia, M.D., Khazar Momeni, and Mahnaz Tayarani
Court Reporter: Nancy Presant CSR #: 9906 Email: npresantcsr@gmail.com, Tel:510-757-8550.

Matter not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

2 - DEFENDANTS DINO ELYASSNIA, KHAZAR MOMENI, MAHNAZ TAYARANI'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiffs' references to punitive damages is GRANTED with leave to amend.

Civil Code section 3294, subdivision (a) restricts punitive damage awards to situations where the defendant has been guilty of "oppression, fraud or malice." Here, Plaintiffs plead facts indicating malice by Defendant Nima Momeni but not for Moving Defendants Khazar Momeni, Dino Elyassnia, or Mahnaz Tayarani. Plaintiffs contend that the alleged concealment of evidence constitutes malice, but they do not plead facts showing that Moving Defendants' conduct was intended to injure Plaintiffs or was "despicable conduct" done with a "willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." (College Hospital, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704.) Plaintiffs' proffered amendments, however, justify leave to amend.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang;Court Reporter: Nancy Presant CSR #: 9906 Email: npresantcsr@gmail.com, Tel:510-757-8550. Reported. (302/SKF)
